text: pink diamonds are some of the rarest
gems in the entire world and there could
only be 500 left out there for us to
discover and I'm talking so rare that
earlier this year one of them sold for

which I mean is nothing compared to the
16.5 billion Daria enur the 186 carat
pink diamond that also happens to be the
largest cut diamond in the world but
where do these diamonds come from and
what gives them their color well nearly
2 billion years ago even before Pangea
there was an ancient supercontinent
named Nuna what makes Nuna so
interesting is that compared to other
supercontinents it was extremely Dynamic
meaning that it broke apart and came
back together a number of times not only
do we think that this violent push and
pull of land mass gave us the pink
diamonds but it also would have given us
our atmosphere you see as Nuna formed
there was a significant increase in
volcanic activity which released vast
amounts of carbon dioxide into the Sky
playing a pivotal role in shaping the
planet's early atmosphere 90 of the pink
diamonds that were ever found were
located at the argyle mine in Western
Australia and researchers recently
discovered they could be up to 1.3
billion years old
